response:
<<Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo to wash her hair every other day. She buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles.>>  
<<She needs to calculate how many bottles she needs for a leap year. The number of weeks in a leap year is 52 weeks plus one day, so she needs 53 weeks of shampoo. Each week, she uses 1/4 ounce twice, so that's 1/2 ounce per week.>>  
<<To find out how many bottles she needs, she divides the total amount of shampoo needed by the capacity of each bottle. The total amount of shampoo needed is 53 weeks × 1/2 ounce per week = 26.5 ounces. Dividing this by 14 ounces per bottle gives her 1.89 bottles. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs 2 bottles.>>  
<<The final answer is [2 bottles] and [53 weeks] to ensure she has enough shampoo for the leap year.>>  
<<cost>>
